page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the ecs.m2 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs, resulting in 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. There are no integrated GPUs, and the configuration includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.m2.medium is classified as a memory-optimized instance. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention. This profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, providing a dedicated CPU allocation with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not contain any local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. Due to its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without proportional compute scaling. It is suitable for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise application servers.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated compute cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
